title: Rich Text Editor - Basic Formatting
description: Verify that users can apply and remove basic text formatting options including bold, italic, underline, and strikethrough in the rich-text editor.

steps.description: Open the rich-text editor page.
Type sample text into the editor.
Apply bold formatting to a portion of the text.
Check that the selected text appears bold.
Apply italic formatting to a portion of the text.
Check that the selected text appears italicized.
Apply underline formatting to a portion of the text.
Check that the selected text is underlined.
Apply strikethrough formatting to a portion of the text.
Check that the selected text has strikethrough applied.
Remove each formatting from the text.
Verify that the text formatting is removed accordingly.

title: Rich Text Editor - Headings and Lists
description: Ensure users can insert headings (H1-H6) and both ordered and unordered lists, and that these elements are rendered correctly in the editor.

steps.description: Open the rich-text editor page.
Insert headings from H1 to H6 and enter sample text for each.
Confirm each heading is rendered with appropriate size and style.
Insert an ordered list with at least 3 items.
Verify the ordered list displays numeric markers correctly.
Insert an unordered list with at least 3 items.
Verify the unordered list displays bullet points correctly.
Test changing list indentations and levels.
Check that nested lists render and behave correctly.

title: Rich Text Editor - Text Alignment
description: Validate that text alignment options (left, center, right, justify) can be applied and are visually reflected within the editor.

steps.description: Open the rich-text editor page and enter multiple paragraphs.
Apply left alignment to first paragraph.
Verify the first paragraph is left aligned.
Apply center alignment to second paragraph.
Verify the second paragraph is center aligned.
Apply right alignment to third paragraph.
Verify the third paragraph is right aligned.
Apply justify alignment to fourth paragraph.
Verify the fourth paragraph is justified.

title: Rich Text Editor - Inline Image Insertion
description: Verify that users can insert inline images into the editor, images are displayed correctly, and remain editable within the document.

steps.description: Open the editor and place cursor at desired location.
Insert an inline image via the editor toolbar.
Verify the image appears inline at the cursor location.
Insert multiple images with varying sizes.
Verify all images are displayed properly and resizable if supported.
Attempt to delete and reposition inserted images.
Ensure images can be removed or moved within the document.

title: Integrity Status Indicator - Typing Speed Tracking
description: Confirm that the system accurately tracks and displays real-time typing speed (words per minute) and updates the indicator color-coded feedback accordingly.

steps.description: Open the editor and begin typing at a slow pace.
Verify the WPM value updates and the integrity status color reflects low speed (likely green or yellow).
Increase typing speed to a moderate level.
Confirm the WPM counter updates to the new speed and status color changes appropriately.
Type rapidly above the typical threshold.
Check that the status indicator shows red or alert color indicating unusual speed.
Pause typing and resume.
Ensure typing speed metric resets or reflects current pace accurately.

title: Integrity Status Indicator - Paste Event Detection
description: Verify that paste events within the editor are detected in real-time and trigger the integrity status indicator changes with correct color coding.

steps.description: Open the editor and paste a block of text from clipboard.
Verify that the paste detection event is triggered immediately.
Confirm that the integrity status indicator changes color (e.g., to red) to reflect paste detection.
After paste, continue typing normally.
Check if the indicator reverts or updates according to typing speed and paste event timing.
Attempt to paste multiple times in quick succession.
Ensure all paste events are detected and indicated.

title: DOCX Export - Formatting and Image Preservation
description: Validate that client-side DOCX export correctly preserves all text formatting, headings, lists, text alignment, and embedded inline images as per the document in the editor.

steps.description: Create a document in the editor with various formatting: headings H1-H3, ordered and unordered lists, bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, text alignment, and embedded images.
Initiate DOCX export client-side.
Verify the exported DOCX file downloads successfully.
Open the DOCX file in standard word processing software.
Check that all formatting matches the editor content exactly.
Confirm that all images appear inline and with correct positioning and sizing.
Verify that lists and nested lists are rendered properly without formatting loss.

title: PDF Export - Print-Quality Fidelity
description: Ensure server-side PDF export returns a print-quality PDF that closely matches the original DOCX content with all formatting, images, and styling preserved.

steps.description: Create a document with complex formatting and images in the editor.
Export the document as DOCX.
Initiate server-side PDF export by uploading DOCX to the API.
Verify PDF export completes within 5 seconds for typical document size.
Download and open the generated PDF file.
Check that PDF content matches DOCX content in text, formatting, headings, lists, alignment, and images.
Confirm print-quality output and consistent styling across sample devices and PDF viewers.

title: Privacy Compliance - Behavioral Data Collection
description: Verify that only aggregate behavioral metadata such as typing speed and paste event counts are collected locally and no sensitive clipboard or content data is transmitted or stored externally.

steps.description: Open the editor and write, copy, and paste content multiple times.
Verify no clipboard content or textual data is sent in any network requests or stored remotely.
Monitor local storage and memory usage for behavioral metadata only.
Confirm that only anonymized, aggregate behavioral signals like WPM and paste counts are stored temporarily on the client.
Simulate offline mode and verify editor behavioral tracking continues without network activity.
Ensure no data leakage occurs to external servers.

title: Distraction-Free UI - Responsiveness and Focus Mode
description: Validate that the writing interface offers a minimal and distraction-free UI optimized for focus and works responsively across desktop and tablet devices.

steps.description: Open the editor on desktop browser.
Confirm minimal UI controls are visible and the editor area occupies most screen space.
Activate the focus/exam mode.
Check that non-essential UI elements are hidden and only writing controls and status indicators remain.
Resize or open editor on tablet device or emulated viewport.
Ensure UI elements adapt correctly and editor remains usable with no layout breaks.
Verify toolbar and integrity status chip is visible and unobtrusive on all device sizes.

title: System Performance - Editor Responsiveness Under Load
description: Test editor input responsiveness and export operation speed under typical and large document sizes to ensure performance criteria are met.

steps.description: Open the editor and input a document with 1000+ words and multiple images.
Verify typing latency remains minimal and UI updates appear instantly.
Export the document to DOCX format.
Confirm export finishes within 5 seconds.
Export the document to PDF format.
Confirm PDF export process completes within 5 seconds.
